items.title: Text classification with thousands of token counts
items.scenario: A spam detector has 20,000 word-count features but only 2,000 labeled emails. Training a dense logistic model on all columns overfits; most tokens appear rarely.
items.explanation: Apply a filter: compute chi-square or mutual information between each token and the spam label, keep the top 500 tokens, then train logistic regression. You reduced input dimension while keeping human-readable word features.

items.title: Medical tabular data with correlated labs
items.scenario: A hospital readmission model has 80 lab measurements; many are highly correlated (different liver enzymes). The team needs a short feature list clinicians can audit.
items.explanation: Use RFE with a regularized logistic regression base estimator and 5-fold CV to find 15 features. Compare held-out AUC against using all 80. If correlated labs swap in and out across folds, treat importances as unstable and involve domain experts.

items.title: House price regression with mixed signal
items.scenario: A housing dataset has square footage, lot size, year built, and 40 neighborhood dummies. Lasso with cross-validated α selects a sparse linear subset for interpretable pricing drivers.
items.explanation: L1 path: `LassoCV` fits many α values; coefficients for weak dummies shrink to zero. Remaining non-zero terms form the selected feature set. Ridge on the same data would keep all dummies with small weights—selection vs shrinkage tradeoff.
